66f1cab1e44f1dc412c3d7b0.question: A vision in the Third Apocalypse of Baruch depicts people associated with this object in canine form, including a woman who gave birth and immediately continued working as if nothing had happened. Rabbinical literature suggests the discovery that arrows returned covered with blood after being shot into the sky justified this object’s creation. The Auricept na n-Éces, or “Scholar’s Primer,” argues that Fénius Farsaid created (*) Gaelic after studying an event that occurred at this object. Instead of the traditional stone and mortar, brick and tar were used for the construction of this object, which some scholars have compared with the ziggurat Etemenanki..This structure’s name may derive from a local word meaning “gate of God.” This structure, which was located in the plain of Shinar, is thought by some to have been built during the reign of Nimrod. This likely mythical building was designed to reach the heavens. For 10 points, name this building whose construction led God to confuse the language of humanity.
